query: Half my eww widgets suddenly lost all their styling — everything below a certain point in eww.scss renders unstyled, even though the top of the file looks fine. The last thing I added was `spacing: 4px;` to one of my box classes to space out the children. Why did that break everything?
expected_behavior: Identifies spacing as a yuck widget attribute, NOT a GTK CSS property
Explains an invalid property causes a parse error that stops all CSS loading from that line onward, silently breaking every widget styled below it
Tells the user to set :spacing in the yuck file instead, and use margin on children (e.g. .box > * { margin: 0 4px; }) for CSS-side spacing
Lists the other yuck-only attributes that must never appear in SCSS (halign, valign, hexpand, vexpand, space-evenly, orientation)
Suggests eww inspector / eww logs to confirm the parse error
notes: Classic confusion between yuck layout attributes and GTK CSS properties. Writing spacing/halign/valign/hexpand in .scss is a parse error that kills all rules below it, so the symptom (everything below breaks) looks unrelated to the cause. A naive answer would debug the broken widgets individually instead of removing the one invalid line and moving spacing to yuck.
